On August 6, 2014, Mr. Malkoski met with Mr. Fortnum, at which meeting Mr. Fortnum indicated Ingredion was prepared to submit a proposal to acquire the Company. Mr. Fortnum suggested that a price of $17.00 per share on a fully diluted basis could likely get support from Ingredion’s board.

On August 10, 2014, Penford received a letter from Mr. Fortnum, confirming Ingredion’s interest in a possible business combination between Ingredion and the Company, citing certain strategic reasons for the proposed acquisition, and providing an indicative valuation of $18.00 per share in cash for all of the outstanding capital stock of Penford on a fully diluted basis.

event_claims.description: Ingredion advised Penford of its interest in acquiring Penford.
quote_texts: On July 17, 2014, Mr. Malkoski and Ms. Gordon met for lunch, during which meeting Ms. Gordon advised Mr. Malkoski of Ingredion’s interest in acquiring Penford.

event_claims.description: Penford's board authorized management and Deutsche Bank to proceed with the market check process.
quote_texts: The board also authorized management and Deutsche Bank to proceed with the market check process as described at the meeting.

event_claims.description: Ingredion indicated it was prepared to submit a proposal to acquire Penford at $17.00 per share on a fully diluted basis.
quote_texts: On August 6, 2014, Mr. Malkoski met with Mr. Fortnum, at which meeting Mr. Fortnum indicated Ingredion was prepared to submit a proposal to acquire the Company. Mr. Fortnum suggested that a price of $17.00 per share on a fully diluted basis could likely get support from Ingredion’s board.

event_claims.description: Penford received Ingredion's letter confirming interest in a possible business combination and providing an indicative valuation of $18.00 per share in cash.
quote_texts: On August 10, 2014, Penford received a letter from Mr. Fortnum, confirming Ingredion’s interest in a possible business combination between Ingredion and the Company, citing certain strategic reasons for the proposed acquisition, and providing an indicative valuation of $18.00 per share in cash for all of the outstanding capital stock of Penford on a fully diluted basis.

event_claims.description: Penford and Ingredion executed a Secrecy Agreement effective July 20, 2014 for limited exchange of confidential information and confidentiality of discussions.
quote_texts: Subsequently, Penford and Ingredion executed a Secrecy Agreement, effective as of July 20, 2014, providing for limited exchange of confidential information and obligating the companies to keep their discussions confidential in order to facilitate the providing of confidential information of Penford to Ingredion.

event_claims.description: Ingredion and Penford executed a nondisclosure and standstill agreement that superseded the prior Secrecy Agreement.
quote_texts: On August 21, 2014, Ingredion and Penford executed a nondisclosure and standstill agreement that superseded the prior Secrecy Agreement.

event_claims.description: Party B informed Deutsche Bank that it had decided not to move forward with discussions or sign a nondisclosure agreement with respect to a potential transaction.
quote_texts: On September 12, 2014, Party B informed Deutsche Bank that it had decided not to move forward with discussions or sign a nondisclosure agreement with respect to a potential transaction.

event_claims.description: Deutsche Bank left voicemails for Party E regarding a potential transaction and received no response.
quote_texts: In the following two weeks, Deutsche Bank also left several other voicemails for Party E regarding a potential transaction and received no response.

Throughout early- to mid-September, in accordance with the board’s authorization, Deutsche Bank contacted six potential strategic counterparties in the same or similar industries to gauge initial interest regarding a potential transaction with Penford.

On September 9, 2014, Party A expressed interest in having further discussions regarding a transaction and Deutsche Bank forwarded Party A a nondisclosure and standstill agreement. Also on September 9, 2014, another strategic counterparty contacted by Deutsche Bank (referred to as Party B) expressed interest in further discussions, and Deutsche Bank forwarded a nondisclosure and standstill agreement to proceed with additional discussions with Party B.

On September 10, 2014, a third potential strategic counterparty contacted by Deutsche Bank (referred to as Party C) expressed interest in pursuing a transaction, and Deutsche Bank forwarded Party C a nondisclosure and standstill agreement on September 11, 2014.

On September 11, 2014, a fourth potential strategic counterparty contacted by Deutsche Bank (referred to as Party D) expressed interest, and Deutsche Bank forwarded a nondisclosure and standstill agreement to Party D.
